summary: The UWSC supports all students with reading, writing, presenting, and creating to learn and communicate. We also contribute directly to the English Corequisite program and support international students with language placement and language learning.

name: UNR Audio Engineering Club
summary: This student-led club is designed to promote appreciation for the art and science of recording, mixing and music production. We provide recording science, music, and engineering students at UNR an opportunity to network and learn from each other.

name: UNR Best Buddies
summary: Best Buddies International is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, leadership development, and inclusive living.

name: UNR Bridge Club
summary: If you love strategy and card games then bridge club is for you. Bridge is a card game that develops critical thinking, creativity, will help you get connections for your career and you can get new friends! We also play a lot of other card games!

name: UNR Chemistry Department
summary: Chemistry is the central science; its principles operate in all aspects of our daily lives. Understanding chemistry is fundamental in understanding the world around us, everything from energy to biology, medicine to materials, and more.

name: UNR Civics Club
summary: UNR Civics is dedicated to getting students to engage in politics and civic life. By spreading information, and encouraging discussion we increase non-partisan political engagement from local government, to international policy.

name: UNR Cricket Club
summary: The organization aim at gathering together cricket enthusiastic people to have cricket activities. We also encourage the students who wish to get to know more about this game-Cricket to be part of the activities of the club

name: UNR Department of Economics
summary: The UNR Economics Department provides academic programs and events that enhance economic understanding.

name: UNR Ducks Unlimited Club
summary: A student chapter of Ducks Unlimited focused on the conservation of wetland habitat via fundraising and field work.

name: UNR Economics Club
summary: The Economics Club provides information to all those interested in studying Economics at the undergraduate level and beyond about opportunities available.Club events and forums help students explore possible career paths, grow econ knowledge and network.

name: UNR Engineering Career Services
summary: Part of the College of Engineering Dean's office to support student career development.

name: UNR Innevation Center
summary: A collaborative space created to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship. We are committed to growing a vibrant entrepreneurial ecosystem in Northern Nevada and expanding our culture of innovation.

name: UNR Institute for Neuroscience
summary: The UNR Institute for Neuroscience is the organizational home of neuroscience research, training, and outreach for neuroscience.

name: UNR Mathematics Club
summary: A hub for enthusiasts of math, both majors and non-majors, to meet friends, network, prepare for grad school, and other activities on math or math adjacent paths

name: UNR Med Classified Collective
summary: Group for classified staff in the UNR School of Medicine

name: UNR Music Teachers Association
summary: The purpose of this organization is to advance the value and study of music making to society and to support the professionalism of music teachers. The official collegiate chapter of the Music Teachers Association at UNR, part of Nevada MTA and MTNA.

name: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate
summary: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate is a club that offers Music Education majors the opportunity to learn more about our field, connect with the community, organize events and build relationships within the club.

name: UNR People Power
summary: ACLU People Power at the University of Nevada, Reno is a student organization focused on advocating for civil liberties and social justice. We engage in activism, educational events, and community outreach to promote and protect individual rights.

name: UNR Robotics
summary: UNR Robotics hopes to create opportunities for its members to gain exposure in the robotics field through the club activities.

name: UNR Veteran Support Team
summary: The Veteran Support team will focus on creating a sense of community for the campus-wide faculty and staff veterans along with connecting to the  Northern Nevada community services and resources.

name: Upward Bound
summary: Four federally funded grant programs providing free college preparatory services to 246 first-generation, income-qualified students annually at eight target high schools in Washoe and Lyon counties.

name: Video Game Club at Lake Tahoe
summary: Video Game Club on the Lake Tahoe Campus provides students with a place to socialize, compete, and have fun with their favorite video games.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at the University of Nevada, Reno seeks to promote the rich culture and heritage of Vietnam within the campus community. Our mission is to foster a sense of unity and support among Vietnamese and non-Vietnamese

name: Welcome Center, Office for Prospective Students
summary: Home to the University campus visit operation, we offer tours and visit programs to prospective students and their families.

name: Western Association of Student Financial Aid Administrators
summary: WASFAA is a community of more than 1,000 financial aid administrators and professionals dedicated to helping students reach their postsecondary education goals. We represent professionals in 8 states in the Western Region.

name: Wolf Pack Racing
summary: Wolf Pack Racking is a club where the students design, manufacture, and race a single-seat off-road race car against other colleges worldwide.

name: Wolf Pack Trumpet Club
summary: The Wolf Pack Trumpet Club is a student led organization dedicated to building strong campus and community connections through trumpet playing.

name: WolfCard Office
summary: The WolfCard can be used to access many on-campus services including meal swipes, campus purchases, access to the Recreation Center, checking out materials in the Mathewson-IGT Knowledge Center, libraries, and other services.

name: Women In Aviation
summary: The mission of the Women in Aviation Club to promote the advancement of women in all aviation and aerospace career fields and interests, provide networking and mentorship opportunities, support professional development, and foster a welcoming community

name: Women in Mining University of Nevada, Reno
summary: WIM-UNR, is a club created for educational purposes that guides its members toward the knowledge of mineral resources and related industries, advocates for a more diverse, equitable, inclusive industry and promote professional development and networking.

name: Young Democrats of University of Nevada Reno
summary: The Young Democrats of UNR exist to engage and inform young people on democratic and progressive issues. We work heavily with local and national organizations to advocate for equity and justice in our community.
